# App

Slim facade — lifecycle + window-level concerns.

# App { #hwpapi.core.app.App }

```python
core.app.App(new_app=False, is_visible=True, dll_path=None, engine=None)
```

High-level v2 façade for HWP (한글) automation.

The slim v2 :class:`App` owns an :class:`~hwpapi.low.engine.Engine`
and exposes only lifecycle + window-level concerns. All
document-scoped operations live on :attr:`doc`.

## Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type           | Description                                                                                                   | Default   |
|------------|----------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| new_app    | bool           | If ``True`` spawn a fresh engine; otherwise reuse the most recently opened one (or create one if none exist). | `False`   |
| is_visible | bool           | Initial visibility of the HWP main window.                                                                    | `True`    |
| dll_path   | str \| None    | Path to ``FilePathCheckerModuleExample.dll``. Auto-discovered when omitted.                                   | `None`    |
| engine     | Engine \| None | Pre-built engine to wrap. Rare — primarily for tests.                                                         | `None`    |

## Examples {.doc-section .doc-section-examples}

```python
>>> with App() as app:
...     app.open("report.hwp")
...     app.doc.text                 # document text (→ Document)
...     app.save_as("report.pdf")
```

## Attributes

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [api](#hwpapi.core.app.App.api) | Read-only COM handle — thin alias for ``self.engine.impl``. |
| [doc](#hwpapi.core.app.App.doc) | Cached :class:`hwpapi.document.Document` façade bound to this App. |
| [visible](#hwpapi.core.app.App.visible) | HWP main-window visibility — read/write. |

## Methods

| Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [close](#hwpapi.core.app.App.close) | Close the active document (``FileClose`` command). |
| [new](#hwpapi.core.app.App.new) | Create an :class:`App` backed by a brand-new HWP document. |
| [open](#hwpapi.core.app.App.open) | Open a document in HWP. Returns the absolute path opened. |
| [quit](#hwpapi.core.app.App.quit) | Terminate the HWP engine (``FileQuit`` command). |
| [reload](#hwpapi.core.app.App.reload) | Rebind this :class:`App` to a fresh/updated engine. |
| [save](#hwpapi.core.app.App.save) | Save the active document. |
| [save_as](#hwpapi.core.app.App.save_as) | v2 rename of v1 ``save_block``: save the active document |

### close { #hwpapi.core.app.App.close }

```python
core.app.App.close(save=False)
```

Close the active document (``FileClose`` command).

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                                                                              | Default   |
|--------|--------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------|
| save   | bool   | If ``True`` save before closing. HWP prompts by default; the caller can set this to force the save path. | `False`   |

### new { #hwpapi.core.app.App.new }

```python
core.app.App.new(is_visible=True)
```

Create an :class:`App` backed by a brand-new HWP document.

v2 rename of v1 ``App.new_document(is_tab=True)``. Replaces the
document-level helper with a constructor-shaped factory so the
returned object is the usual App handle.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name       | Type   | Description                                  | Default   |
|------------|--------|----------------------------------------------|-----------|
| is_visible | bool   | Initial window visibility. Default ``True``. | `True`    |

#### Returns {.doc-section .doc-section-returns}

| Name   | Type   | Description                                                |
|--------|--------|------------------------------------------------------------|
|        | App    | Fresh :class:`App` bound to a brand-new engine + document. |

### open { #hwpapi.core.app.App.open }

```python
core.app.App.open(path, format=None, arg='')
```

Open a document in HWP. Returns the absolute path opened.

#### Parameters {.doc-section .doc-section-parameters}

| Name   | Type        | Description                                                                                                    | Default    |
|--------|-------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| path   | str \| Path | File path (relative paths are resolved against cwd).                                                           | _required_ |
| format | str         | Forwarded to ``HwpObject.Open`` when provided (HWP's extended-open signature). Omit for the default behaviour. | `None`     |
| arg    | str         | Forwarded to ``HwpObject.Open`` when provided (HWP's extended-open signature). Omit for the default behaviour. | `None`     |

### quit { #hwpapi.core.app.App.quit }

```python
core.app.App.quit()
```

Terminate the HWP engine (``FileQuit`` command).

### reload { #hwpapi.core.app.App.reload }

```python
core.app.App.reload(new_app=False, dll_path=None)
```

Rebind this :class:`App` to a fresh/updated engine.

Useful after DLL reinstall or when the HWP process has exited
out from under the Python binding. Also invalidates the cached
:attr:`doc`.

### save { #hwpapi.core.app.App.save }

```python
core.app.App.save(path=None, format=None, arg='')
```

Save the active document.

Without ``path`` performs an in-place save (``HwpObject.Save``).
With ``path`` performs a ``SaveAs`` using the file-extension-
derived format — ``.hwp``, ``.pdf``, ``.hwpx``, ``.hml``, ``.png``,
``.txt``, ``.docx`` are recognised.

### save_as { #hwpapi.core.app.App.save_as }

```python
core.app.App.save_as(path, format=None, arg='')
```

v2 rename of v1 ``save_block``: save the active document
selection/block to ``path``.

Extension-sniffed format set matches v1: ``.hwp``, ``.pdf``,
``.hwpx`` (HWPML2X), ``.png``.
